decision_summary: The chip temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the limit (85.0°C). The previous attempt to increase spreader conductivity failed due to exceeding the upper bound (500.0). The current conductivity is 425.25. I will increase it to the maximum allowed value of 500.0 to maximize lateral heat spreading without violating bounds.

changes.reason: Maximizing spreader conductivity within the allowed bound [20.0, 500.0] improves lateral heat distribution, reducing the peak temperature on the chip.

expected_effects: Reduction in chip_max_temperature towards the 85.0°C constraint
More uniform temperature distribution across the heat spreader
risk_notes: This change hits the upper property bound; if 500.0 W/(m*K) is insufficient, geometric changes (width/height) will be required in the next step.
Diminishing returns may occur as conductivity approaches the theoretical maximum for the material class.
